role: Senior adviser and chief political strategist to President Ahmadinejad
bio:
Mojtaba Samareh Hashemi was the chief political strategist and senior adviser to President [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad), often described as a cross between 'Iran's Karl Rove and a president's chief of staff.' He studied with Ahmadinejad at Iran University of Science and Technology (IUST) in the late 1970s and maintained close ties to the [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) and intelligence services.

Samareh Hashemi is a nephew of [Mohammad-Javad Bahonar](/people/mohammad-javad-bahonar), the prime minister assassinated in 1981. A dedicated follower of [Mohammad-Taqi Mesbah-Yazdi](/people/mohammad-taqi-mesbah-yazdi), he was placed in the Foreign Ministry as director of placements in the early 1990s on Mesbah-Yazdi's sponsorship. He later served as deputy interior minister for political affairs from 2006.

Samareh Hashemi played a central role in Ahmadinejad's election campaigns and inner-circle decision-making, and was considered one of the most powerful behind-the-scenes figures in the administration.

sources.title: All Ahmadinejad's Men — Middle East Forum

editorial_notes: IUST classmate of Ahmadinejad. Nephew of assassinated PM Bahonar. Strong IRGC/intelligence ties. Mesbah-Yazdi protege.
